summary: ACCEPT: R3 systems architecture review accepts M4 withdrawal and postwar handoff. The prior objective-complete and grain-ledger blockers are fixed: objective-complete now rejects null march references, requires a completed campaign, surrendered siege, matching target, and matching march before creating WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate; the orderly-withdrawal return ledger now has stable source and reservation-field tie-breakers; regressions cover null-march rejection with no outcomes/candidates and deterministic shared-reservation return allocation. The implementation remains scoped to M4 withdrawal/result DTO handoff into accepted M3 governance boundaries and does not add excluded systems.

tests_run.result: On feat/m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff. Active worktree before this ACCEPT handoff contained R3 changes in packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, tests/sim-core-m4-withdrawal-postwar-handoff.test.ts, allowed coordination state, and untracked writer REVIEW-R3 handoff.
Branch diff is limited to packages/protocol, packages/sim-core, tests, and allowed project task/message/route/model state files. No forbidden tracked path was modified.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-only origin/main | rg <forbidden path pattern>
tests_run.result: No forbidden-path matches; rg exits 1 when no match is found.
Vitest passed: 1 file, 11 tests, including null march objective-complete rejection and deterministic shared-reservation return allocation.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 32 files and 223 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Task JSON validated successfully in REVIEW state.
Writer REVIEW-R3 handoff JSON validated successfully.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for task and REVIEW-R3 handoff
tests_run.result: Task JSON and writer REVIEW-R3 handoff parsed successfully.

decisions: Accepted objective-complete gating because WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate creation now requires completed campaign state, surrendered siege result, target match, explicit march reference, and siege.marchId equal to the resolved march.marchId.

Accepted the source-ledger withdrawal accounting because orderly returned grain is allocated by grain reservation source ledger with stable reservation/source/field tie-breakers and regression coverage for shared reservationId rows.
Accepted WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate query boundary copying because nested reasonCodes and validM3Methods arrays are copied before exposure and aliasing regression coverage passes.
Accepted failed-extraction coverage because the deterministic loss-trigger path records failed-extraction, human-loss reason codes, and troop conservation.

No hard-exclusion implementation was found: no full diplomacy, peace treaties, rebellion system, formal historical content, campaign planning UI, M5 content, manual node battle, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, save-format package change, or parallel postwar settlement model.
risks: Residual R3 risk is normal integration risk for authoritative M4 command/state/hash behavior; local sim-core, protocol, determinism, architecture, and full project tests passed.
Save-format package remains intentionally untouched per task scope; any future persisted compatibility work must be routed through an explicit save/schema task.
requested_action: Lead may run integration gates and proceed with task acceptance workflow for M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001.
